title: FY 2025 Popular Annual Financial Report
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 2, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Vicki Van Buren, Director of Finance
Matt Taylor, Assistant Finance Director
DEPT: Finance
SUBJECT: FY 2025 Popular Annual Financial Report

Vicki Van Buren and Matt Taylor are writing to inform you that the City of Reno has completed and submitted the City of Reno's Popular Annual Financial Report (PAFR) for the fiscal year 2025 to the Government Finance Officers Association (GFOA) for the Government Finance Officers Association's review and consideration.

The PAFR serves as an accessible and user-friendly summary of the City of Reno's financial position and activities, designed to communicate key financial data in a clear and engaging manner for the general public. The PAFR includes information on the City of Reno's financial performance, major initiatives, and the state of the local economy, providing an overview that is intended to be informative to a broad audience, including residents, taxpayers, and community stakeholders.

The City of Reno's submission to the Government Finance Officers Association is part of the Finance Department's ongoing commitment to transparency and accountability in financial reporting. The Government Finance Officers Association's PAFR award program encourages governments to produce high-quality reports that present financial data in an easily understandable way. By submitting the Popular Annual Financial Report, the City of Reno is not only striving to meet industry best practices but also ensuring that the City of Reno continues to improve the City of Reno's public communication and strengthen the trust of the community.

Should the City of Reno be awarded the Government Finance Officers Association's PAFR recognition, the Government Finance Officers Association's PAFR recognition will further demonstrate the City of Reno's dedication to financial transparency and the responsible management of public resources. The Government Finance Officers Association's PAFR recognition will also be the fourth year of the award and City of Reno will qualify as a Triple Crown Winner for achievement in Financial Reporting for the Budget Book, ACFR and PAFR. The PAFR is also available on the City of Reno's website, https://www.reno.gov/government/departments/finance.

If you have any questions regarding the submission or would like to discuss the contents of the PAFR, please feel free to contact the Finance Department's office.

fields.authors: Vicki Van Buren, Director of Finance; Matt Taylor, Assistant Finance Director
fields.tldr: The City of Reno's Finance Department has completed and submitted the FY 2025 Popular Annual Financial Report (PAFR) to the Government Finance Officers Association (GFOA) for review. The PAFR is a public-friendly summary of the city's financial position, major initiatives, and local economic conditions. If the GFOA award is received, it will mark the fourth consecutive year of recognition and qualify Reno as a Triple Crown Winner in financial reporting, covering the Budget Book, ACFR, and PAFR. The report is publicly available on the City of Reno's Finance Department website.
fields.key_stats: Fourth consecutive year of potential GFOA PAFR award recognition; Triple Crown Winner status at stake covering Budget Book, ACFR, and PAFR achievements.
fields.key_decisions: No council action required; memo is informational, notifying the Mayor and City Council of the PAFR submission to the GFOA.
fields.action_items: No formal action items identified; Finance Department invites questions and is available for discussion regarding the PAFR submission.

projects: Triple Crown Winner – Financial Reporting (Budget Book, ACFR, PAFR)
people: Vicki Van Buren, Director of Finance
